$comment: Deckback runtime config. Read by launcher/. Designed to be remotely overridable and signed (doc §6 P10 / risk R1) so Leanback breakage can be hotfixed without a rebuild. UA is the single biggest external dependency (S0.2) — keep it hot-swappable.
$comment_user_agent: Use the Cobalt TV client form. Desktop/SteamDeck tokens select a different YouTube TV style bundle and make overlay text backgrounds opaque.

quality.$comment: steer_av1_unsupported: launcher injects a CDP script (navigator.cpp) making AV1 report unsupported across MediaSource.isTypeSupported / canPlayType / mediaCapabilities.decodingInfo, so YouTube serves VP9/H.264. AV1 hardware decode on the Deck is unproven/disputed (findings durable/hardware.md) — steering stays until that's settled. Patch-free.

$comment_mic: Phase 5: launcher auto-grants the mic (Browser.grantPermissions audioCapture) for the app origin only, so voice search needs no permission prompt in Game Mode.
$comment_cdm: Phase 7 (best-effort, opt-in): leave empty to never fetch — the launcher only detects an already-installed Widevine CDM. To enable DRM/paid content, set cdm_url to a raw libwidevinecdm.so you trust and cdm_sha256 to its digest; the launcher verifies the hash before installing into the profile. The CDM is never redistributed by this project (docs/legal.md). Free YouTube is unaffected either way.

power.$comment: Phase 6 (doc §2 P6). Play-state is polled over DevTools (needs remote_debugging_port) to hold the logind idle inhibitor while a video plays. Synthetic fallback is off by default.
power.$comment_keep_awake: SteamOS dims the screen and auto-suspends mid-video unless the host-side keep-awake helper (scripts/install-idle-nudge.sh, `just idle-nudge`) is running — nothing inside the Flatpak sandbox can prevent it (findings durable/keep-awake.md). When true, the launcher asks the host user systemd whether deckback-idle-nudge.service is active, once, on the first tick that observes playback, and shows a one-time toast if it is not. It stays silent when the answer is unknown (e.g. the sandbox may not talk to org.freedesktop.systemd1), so it can never nag about something it cannot see. Set false to suppress the toast if you deliberately run without the helper.
power.$comment_reload: If the Deck slept at least this many ms, reload on resume (Page.reload) instead of just nudging play(), so stale stream URLs / auth token are refreshed. Measured with CLOCK_BOOTTIME. 0 = disabled (always nudge). Tune on-Deck; e.g. 3600000 = 1h.
power.$comment_self_update: Self-update policy via the Flatpak portal (findings durable/self-update.md). Three modes: 'off' = the updater never runs, update only via Desktop Mode `flatpak update`/Discover; 'notify' (DEFAULT) = detect a newer commit and surface a passive amber dot + a one-time 'Update available' card (launcher-drawn overlay, so a YouTube frontend change can't break it), but NEVER deploy without the user choosing 'Update now' (reachable any time from the Menu); 'auto' = deploy any newer commit in the background so it binds on the next launch, no prompt. In every mode the launcher pre-grants the portal's consent (permission store flatpak/updates=yes — Game Mode has no Access dialog) and the portal updates ONLY this app from its own `deckback` remote — no root, no password. Updating the runtime and every other flatpak is a separate `flatpak update`. Default is 'notify' so updates are never enforced without consent; note 'auto' has no couch-reachable rollback — a bad published commit reaches every opted-in Deck on next launch. The legacy boolean `self_update` still parses (true -> auto, false -> off).

log.$comment: Size-rotating file sink owned by launcher/ (doc §2 P2). Empty log_directory derives from DECKBACK_LOG_DIR / XDG_STATE_HOME at runtime. Lines also mirror to stderr for journald/Steam.

$comment_cobalt_flags: HARDWARE DECODE IS ENABLED as of M138/cobalt-27 (pixel-verified on-Deck 2026-07-10, OLED). VA-API needs BOTH --enable-features=VaapiVideoDecodeLinuxGL AND --use-angle=gl to engage (with the feature but not the GL backend, ANGLE stays on Vulkan/radv, VA-API never engages, and the pipeline falls back to software WITHOUT SAYING SO). On m114 that exact pair produced VaapiVideoDecoder AND green-band corruption on EVERY frame (ANGLE's DMA-buf/EGLImage import of the tiled NV12 VA surface was broken; corruptedVideoFrames stayed 0 so decoder-name + corrupted-frame checks BOTH false-passed while the panel was striped). On M138 the SAME two flags were re-tested through the shipped flatpak (Game Mode, real Leanback VP9 playback) under the DUAL gate — decoder==VaapiVideoDecoder+kIsPlatformVideoDecoder AND a composited screenshot through tests/deck/lib/probes.py:video_corruption_verdict — and came back VaapiVideoDecoder + 0.0% green-band rows across 5 frames, visually clean: M138's newer ANGLE fixes the import. See findings durable/hardware.md (★ RESOLVED note) and milestones/m114.md for the failure history. If a future Cobalt bump reopens this, RE-CONFIRM with the PIXEL check (screenshot, never kVideoDecoderName / corruptedVideoFrames alone) before trusting these flags, and revert to software decode if it regresses. Do NOT add --ignore-gpu-blocklist or VaapiIgnoreDriverChecks: they change no decoder selection and only suppress real driver checks.

keymap.$comment: Deck control -> semantic action OR a DOM key value dispatched verbatim ('Enter', 'Escape', 'MediaPlayPause', 'c', 'ArrowUp'...). A DOM key wins over an action name, which is what makes this hot-swappable: a Leanback change can be worked around by shipping a new app.json, no rebuild. Consumed by launcher/src/input.cpp. Unmapped actions are logged at startup rather than dispatching a no-op.
keymap.$comment_unmapped: The unmapped actions are NOT one problem (findings durable/input-ux.md §8). scan_rewind/scan_forward have candidate VK codes (MediaRewind 227, MediaFastForward 228, tree-verified in starboard/key.h) but launcher/src/devtools.cpp cannot dispatch them yet, and Leanback's handling of them is unverified. player_menu has no candidate at all. YouTube publishes no key contract for its TV interface -- we refuse to guess. voice_search was removed with the voice feature (never worked on this engine: Cobalt routes voice through a Starboard platform service this Chromium-based build lacks); y is unbound until it returns.
keymap.$comment_start: Menu (start) is the launcher's fixed off-playback Settings entry point. The legacy show_controls value remains here only so older hot-swapped configs are harmless: input.cpp removes it before normal key dispatch, and Menu opens Settings even if this mapping is changed or removed.
keymap.$comment_seek: THREE seek models are available; LB/RB and LT/RT ship bound. scrub_back/scrub_fwd (LB/RB) resolve to ArrowLeft/ArrowRight, which nudge Leanback's own progress bar (the console model). chapter_back/chapter_fwd (LT/RT, the shipped default) do NOT dispatch a key: the launcher evaluates config/scripts/chapter_seek.js over CDP, which jumps to the prev/next CHAPTER boundary. Chapters come from the TVHTML5 /next endpoint's macroMarkersListEntity (verified reachable on-Deck 2026-07-13 -- input-ux §18 ★ CORRECTION; the earlier 'unreachable' claim probed /player, the wrong endpoint). When a video has no chapters, chapter_seek falls back to a fixed ±skip_seconds jump, so LT/RT always do something. For a pure fixed jump with no chapter logic, bind skip_back/skip_fwd instead (same seekBy(±skip_seconds)). skip_seconds (below) tunes the fixed interval / fallback and is hot-swappable, as is chapter_seek.js itself. The old names seek_back_10/seek_fwd_10 still resolve to scrub for one release (so a remote hot-swap does not break) but warn at startup.
keymap.$comment_captions: toggle_captions (View by default) is a LAUNCHER action, NOT a DOM key. It used to resolve to 'c', the desktop youtube.com caption hotkey, which youtube.com/tv (Leanback) receives and ignores -- Google publishes no key contract for the TV app (findings input-ux.md §8.1). The launcher now evaluates config/scripts/toggle_captions.js over CDP, driving the HTML5 player's own caption module (getOption/setOption tracklist+track) like chapter_seek.js drives seeking. The script returns the new state and the launcher shows a brief toast (captions_toast, below). The exact caption module API is version-dependent, so toggle_captions.js is hot-swappable: if a Cobalt/Leanback bump moves it, ship a new script via a config push -- no rebuild. Bind 'captions'/'toggle_captions' to any button to move it.

$comment_layers: Contextual keymap layers (findings input-ux §2/§12), all hot-swappable like keymap itself. keymap_player/keymap_osk are CONTEXT layers, applied when the watch view is up or a text field has focus; they override the base map and unbound controls fall through to it. keymap_lt/keymap_rt are HELD-TRIGGER MODIFIER layers: a non-empty layer turns that trigger into a modifier (it stops dispatching its own key) and ABSORBS controls it does not bind, so LT+A cannot silently fire a plain A. Direction controls (up/down/left/right) are bindable in a layer too. All four ship EMPTY on purpose: no Leanback key goes in here without an on-Deck spike (TASKS.md P3, no-guessing policy). The layer is selected from the play-state poll, so it needs remote_debugging_port.
$comment_error_page: Kiosk failure state (findings input-ux §7). When a navigation fails, Chromium renders a desktop error interstitial whose Reload button no controller can focus, inside an app with no address bar and no keyboard — a hard dead-end. Instead the launcher navigates to about:blank and injects its own page with a focused Try again button (Enter or Space), retrying automatically with exponential backoff from error_retry_min_ms to error_retry_max_ms. Set error_page:false to get Chromium's page back; note the launcher then also stops noticing the failure, because a failed navigation leaves document.location.href set to the URL it tried.
$comment_error_text: error_title and error_hint override the page's wording; empty means derive it from the net:: error. THIS IS THE R1 HOTFIX SURFACE: if Leanback changes under us and stops loading, the explanation reaching users is a config push, not a rebuild. Text is escaped before injection, so quotes and backslashes are safe.
$comment_first_run: One-shot controls card (findings input-ux §17), drawn over CDP once the TV app has loaded. After dismissal, the same live control list is in Settings ▸ Keys, opened with the fixed Menu button. View = captions is unguessable, and SUPPORT.md is invisible from Game Mode. The card is DERIVED FROM THIS FILE, never hardcoded: rebind a control and the card says so; a control that resolves to no key is omitted rather than advertised as dead. The card is modal -- while it is up the launcher swallows every controller event, so nothing moves behind it -- and any button press dismisses it (never a stick, which drifts at rest). Shown-once is recorded in $XDG_STATE_HOME/deckback/first_run_v1; delete that file to see it again.
$comment_right_stick: Fast list traversal on the second axis (findings input-ux §7/§15): the right stick emits the same arrow keys the D-pad does, but its repeat RATE is analog — right_stick_slow_ms at the deadzone edge down to right_stick_fast_ms at full deflection. The D-pad accelerates over time because it is digital and cannot know how hard you are pushing; the right stick must not, because then the ramp and your thumb would fight over the same variable. Suppressed while the D-pad or left stick hold a direction. Arrow keys are the only verified Leanback bindings, so this adds no new key assumption. Set false to leave the stick unused.
$comment_skip_seconds: The ± jump in seconds for a trigger bound to skip_back/skip_fwd (LT/RT by default). The launcher evaluates the player's own seekBy(±skip_seconds, true) over CDP on the trigger's press edge (findings input-ux §18) — a fixed-interval seek, not a progress-bar scrub. Hot-swappable. Only takes effect while the trigger is not repurposed as a modifier layer (a non-empty keymap_lt/keymap_rt wins).
$comment_captions_toast: Confirm each caption toggle (View, keymap toggle_captions) with a brief on-screen toast — 'Captions on'/'Captions off', or 'No captions for this video' when the player exposes no tracks. Off a video the press is a silent no-op. The player is otherwise an unobservable surface (same rationale as the touch-lock toast), so without this a press gives no feedback. Set false to suppress the toast; the toggle still fires. See $comment_captions in keymap.
$comment_caption_control: Master caption kill switch. 'local' (default) = our override: on each video start captions are auto-applied in your preferred-language list, the on/off state carries across videos (seeded from YouTube's own state on the first video), and View toggles it. 'youtube' = defer to YouTube: View is a plain on/off toggle in the system language, no auto-start, no preferred list. Edited in-app via OSD ▸ Settings ▸ Captions; persisted in user.json. NOTE: this controls our LOCAL behaviour only — YouTube's own account-level 'captions on by default' (synced server-side across your devices) is a separate YouTube setting.
$comment_caption_languages: Ordered list of preferred caption languages, tried in order when captions turn ON (authoritative — beats the remembered/ambient track). Entries are language codes ('en', 'de', ...); the special 'system' follows the Steam Deck system language (the launcher reads the LANG/LC_* locale SteamOS sets from Settings ▸ System ▸ Language). Empty falls back to the legacy 'caption_language' scalar, then 'system'. EDITED IN-APP via the OSD ▸ Settings ▸ Captions sub-tab (add/remove languages), which persists changes to user.json — this shipped value is only the default. The launcher matches on the primary subtag, so 'pt' also matches 'pt-BR' tracks.
$comment_caption_type: Which track to prefer within a language: 'author_first' (author-provided > auto-generated > machine translation, the default), 'auto_first' (auto > author > translation), 'author_only' (skip auto/translated), or 'auto_only'. Editable in the OSD Captions sub-tab; persisted in user.json.
$comment_caption_remember: Whether the caption on/off state survives an app restart (persisted in user.json:caption_on). Within a session the on/off state always carries across videos; this only controls cross-restart persistence. Off = each launch re-seeds the on/off state from the first video's own caption state. Editable in the OSD Captions sub-tab ('Remember on/off after restart').
$comment_caption_on: The persisted local caption on/off state (only trusted when caption_remember is on). Written by the launcher on each View toggle / auto-apply — not normally hand-edited.
$comment_caption_language: DEPRECATED single-language scalar, kept for hot-swap compatibility: it seeds caption_languages when that list is empty. Prefer caption_languages (an ordered list) instead.
$comment_disable_touch: Make the touchscreen inert (findings durable/touch-lock.md). On the Deck under gamescope the panel arrives as synthetic MOUSE events — a finger moves the cursor and taps click, navigating YouTube by accident — and the app cannot grab the panel from inside (proven on hardware). disable_touch neutralises touch two ways: (A) the launcher injects config/no_pointer.js to swallow every pointer/mouse/touch event in the page (Leanback is D-pad/key driven, so nothing is lost), and (B) TouchModeGuard holds gamescope's global touch mode at hover (STEAM_TOUCH_CLICK_MODE=0 — cursor moves, no click) WHILE our window is focused, so the Steam overlay's touch is untouched. Verified on-Deck 2026-07-10: at hover, taps produced 45 mousemoves and 0 clicks and did not navigate. Set false to restore raw touch (taps click). no_pointer.js also HIDES the cursor (cursor:none via a constructable stylesheet — gamescope composites our X cursor, so this makes it draw nothing; verified on-Deck the cursor vanishes), so with disable_touch on there is no visible cursor at all.
$comment_touch_lock_removed: The touch_lock_* / block_touchscreen keys are GONE. They configured an EVIOCGRAB touchscreen lock that is PROVEN NON-FUNCTIONAL on SteamOS: the launcher can't open the panel node, and even granted access the grab doesn't block touch because gamescope — not our app — reads the panel (findings durable/touch-lock.md, physical test 2026-07-10). The code is deleted, not shipped disabled. An older app.json that still sets these keys loads fine and is ignored silently (the launcher lists them as known-and-ignored), so a hot-swapped config can't resurrect a dead lock or spray warnings. Do not reintroduce them without a hardware retest of the grab.
